item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Current ending is very weak, as it is. Makes sense in-universe, but really hurts the ending. On top of that, the REDACTEDs don't really add anything and just lazily add mystery. However, even with those two points, I do like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Abort it with FIRE! Embryos are creepy, scratch that, babies in all their stages are creepy, and these little guys just take the take. Take my upvote, you monster.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This has an inline link to the Wanderer's library. I propose linking it to the Serpent's Hand Hub.

Editing this:

It is believed the book was from the Wanderer's Library, and that Watson is currently held prisoner for damage to a Library book.

To this:

It is believed the book was from the Wanderer's Library, and that Watson is currently held prisoner for damage to a Library book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could you remove the redacteds? It's a bit annoying and if you talk to an experienced doctor I'm sure you could find something sufficiently disturbing, also I don't see why the site director wouldn't want his/her name on this, just find a generic name.

Also the bobbit worm was great, big fan of those things, it's like it has convergent evolution with a landmine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's "Secure, Contain and Protect", not "Starve, Complain and Neglect". The Foundation performs the necessary upkeep on everything it holds to the best of its resources and ability because in general it is worried what would happen when skips are permitted to break down, as there are several examples of that in containment as well.

Maybe it will turn to common murky water if left unattended for a year, or maybe it will develop the ecosystem responsible for 093, who knows?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When properly maintained with salt water, SCP-2259 is capable of spontaneously generating human embryos, henceforth referred to as "genesis events."

…

SCP-2259 is to be kept maintained with charcoal filters and fluorescent lights replaced as necessary. The tank will be cleaned carefully to avoid damage to specimens inside. Twice daily fish food will be sprinkled onto the surface of the water.

No specimens can be brought to term without permission from Site Director ████████ and the Ethics Committee.

So if you don't maintain the water, it doesn't produce cannibalistic human chimera fetuses… and the Foundation doesn't need it to produce cannibalistic human chimera fetuses… but they maintain the water and provide food anyway, ensuring that every month or so, hundreds of potential humans will be produced only to be cannibalized and/or drown? That seems both inefficient and unnecessarily cruel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man. The imagery gave me the willie-nillies. Quite sad and depressing, which makes for great fiction. +1.

One question though: why did the bobbit worm give them so much trouble and cause such a disruption in the cycle? Just shoot/stab/incinerate the damn thing already :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: upvoted for the goddamn bobbit worm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, enjoyed entirely.

At this point, though, I'll note that there were enough 'At this point,' paragraphs to stand out to me, and as basically my sole suggestion I guess maybe you could find another introductory phrase or two?

You can tell I have little complaint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very cool idea, I really like the links to classic theories of spontaneous generation. However, this paragraph is a bit awkwardly worded, doesn't flow for me.

During the raid, the suspect destroyed much of an old book. At this point, a number of arms emerged from a cupboard and dragged the suspect from Foundation custody before vanishing. It is believed the book was from the Wanderer's Library, and that Watson is currently held prisoner for damage to a Library book.

Especially "At this point, a number of arms emerged from a cupboard and dragged the suspect from Foundation custody before vanishing."

Aside from that, I really like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this. It's just weird enough to make it entertaining and a unique little snowflake, while also just being an interesting idea itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A skip from an author avatared veteran of the site that's not DrClef? Cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP reminds me some species of sharks, like the Carcharias taurus, but the sharks are more compassionate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, uh:

No specimens can be brought to term without permission from Site Director ████████ and the Ethics Committee.

So no, I don't think the purpose of this is to make D-Class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the period of greatest cannibalism as the fetuses actively hunt for each other

Overall, the article's eh (oh boy, a way to make new D-class!), but there is something truly sublime about this line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh, yes - but by that measure it's also like American Idol, with fetuses and consuming the others flesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. Take this: +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this like the hunger games with fetuses and consuming the others flesh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The note fell flat for enough people that I've removed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a 1000 liter glass fish tank measuing 280cm x 65cm x 55cm

280 cm × 65 cm × 55 cm × 1 l/1000cm3=1001 liters, unless I messed up the math (twice).

I really don't like the final note for the unlikelyhood of "sirpudding" being a Foundation employee's name, the ridiculousness of leaving notes to your children/homoculi in official documents, and the fact that my parents would have told me if I was a homoculus. I find myself downvoting a Mann article, I suppose there's a first time for everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the end is a little gimmicky. I upvoted for the imagery of the tank, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time an article calls me "CirclesAndSquares" I am kicked right out of the narrative. Hard. I think putting that in an article weakens it incredibly. It was a solid +1 until that, at which point I decided no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True the personal note is silly, but this clearly wouldn't work as a -J. Problem is it kind of sits in between -J and mainlister, but that doesn't mean it's not a good piece, or that the final note should be removed. Overall it fits as a mainlister better than it does on the -J list.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the ListUsers module falls flat because it relies on a wikidot name.

Chiming in to say "I agree with this sentiment".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I said I'd upvote it so I have

+1

item.title: Re: Some thoughts
item.content:encoded:
I'm not digging all the expunge.

All the redaction in this one is hinted around and implying something more and used with purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this is an extended dad joke, it should be a -J. If it's not a joke, I don't understand how the personal note exists. And in any case, the ListUsers module falls flat because it relies on a wikidot name.

item.title: Some thoughts
item.content:encoded:
I'll just collapse this, thankyouverymuch
Because there are spoilers, you see.
  1. Holy shit, Yoric and Cherry Pict gave feedback on this? Was this sitting in your sandbox for years, or are you still in touch with them, or…?? Ignore, see edit below.
  2. I'm not digging all the expunge. It's… jarring. Yeah, I know, I'm talking to someone who's been writing skips since the damn project got off the ground, hang me. But still, I feel like there's at least a little bit there that can be put into place instead of the jarring expungements.
  3. The last line is hilarious, and a good use of that damnable module, but it also pushes this into "Maybe better as a joke article" to me.

All around entertaining, but I'm unsure whether or not this should be on the mainlist. It's definitely creepy/funny enough to stick around on the site, but, well, you used the SCP format as setup for an advanced form of a "You're adopted" joke. Not sure where to classify this in my mind because of that.

Pseudo-edit: Mann had to say something regarding #2 in chat, and I found it novel enough to preserve it, here, in the discussion page:

10:57:35 PM DrMann The expungement is to let people imagine for themselves how fetuses might skeletonize a rabbit.

There's my thoughts. Do with them what you will, I hope it's helpful.

Edit: Oh hey, look, there's yoric, I'm an idiot, disregard #1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh damn it, Dad. Get off the internet and back in your hole.

EDIT: If he removes that line, this joke won't make sense…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I feel that the "List User" gimmick feels overused.
The Whole buildup to the last addendum was actually pretty fun.
+1 all day any day
I love those little jumpscares"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to pooryoric and Cherry Pict for helping me develop this article, and thanks to everyone who proof-read and gave me suggestions for improvement.
